text:
Under the vast sea of stars an octopus danced.

It was an opulent dance. Or perhaps it was just a flick of limbs. Either way, it was beautiful. The moonlight bathed it.

This wasn't its usual night though. There were no crabs or moths tonight. Perhaps it wanted something.

It was curious about life.

It wasn't scared; it knew its own strength.

Its feet were long.

And soft.

Like cotton.

It swam slowly.

And carefully.

It touched.

A little.

A squid.

Like unto itself.

That's an odd thing to say.

But that's true.

That squid knew exactly why it was there

And danced.

And dreamed.

And dreamed about...

A boy.




That's an odd thing to say.




But that's true.


Oh.




The dreams:






With hair.



And eyes.



He was.


Oh.




That's a lot.

That's all.




Oh.





Oh!


That's beautiful.






There.
